memoryArtifacts.headline: Warwick Castle, the principal residence of Guy de Beauchamp and Alice de Toeni
memoryArtifacts.description:
Alice de Toeni, Countess of Warwick (26 April 1284 – 1 January 1324/25) was a wealthy English heiress and the second wife of Guy de Beauchamp, 10th Earl of Warwick (1272 – 12 August 1315), an English nobleman in the reign of Kings Edward I and Edward II. He was one of the principal opponents of Piers Gaveston, a favourite of Edward II. Alice married three times; Guy was her second husband.

Family and lineage
Alice de Toeni (or de Tosny) was born on 26 April 1284 in Flamsted, Hertfordshire, the only daughter of Ralph VII de Toeni, Lord Toeni of Flamsted (1255–1295) and his Scottish-born wife, Mary. Alice's paternal grandparents were Roger V de Toeni, Lord Flamsted and Alice de Bohun. The latter was a daughter of Humphrey de Bohun, 2nd Earl of Hereford and Maud de Lusignan. Alice had an older brother Robert de Toeni, Lord Toeni of Flamsted (4 April 1276–1309), who married Maud, the daughter of Malise, 6th Earl of Strathearn, but died childless in 1309. Upon his death, Alice became his heir. Her inheritance included manors in Essex, Worcestershire, Wiltshire, Hertfordshire, Cambridgeshire, and the Welsh Marches.


Warwick Castle, the principal residence of Guy de Beauchamp and Alice de Toeni
Marriages and issue[edit]
In 1300, when Alice was sixteen, she married her first husband, Sir Thomas Leybourne (died May 1307), son of Sir William Leybourne, by whom she had one daughter: Juliana de Leybourne (1303/1304 – 1367), married firstly, John, Lord Hastings, by whom she had issue, secondly Thomas le Blount, and thirdly, William Clinton. On 28 February 1309, less than two years after the death of her first husband, Alice married secondly Guy de Beauchamp, 10th Earl of Warwick, the only son of William de Beauchamp, 9th Earl of Warwick and Maud FitzJohn.[citation needed]

He had been previously married to Isabel de Clare, the daughter of Gilbert de Clare, 6th Earl of Gloucester and Alice de Lusignan of Angoulême, but the marriage, which had produced no children, was annulled. Guy had already distinguished himself in the Scottish Wars and was one of the Ordainers, who sought to restrict the powers of the King. Guy de Beauchamp was one of the chief adversaries of Piers Gaveston, King Edward's favourite, who often referred to Guy as The Mad Hound, due to the Earl's habit of foaming at the mouth when angry. In 1312, Guy de Beauchamp captured Gaveston and took him to his principal residence Warwick Castle where Gaveston was held prisoner and afterwards murdered.[citation needed]

Alice and Guy had two sons and five daughters:

Maud de Beauchamp (died 1366), married Geoffrey de Say, 2nd Lord Say, by whom she had issue.
Isabella de Beauchamp, married John Clinton.
Emma de Beauchamp, married Rowland Odingsells.
Thomas de Beauchamp, 11th Earl of Warwick (14 February 1313/1314 – 13 November 1369), married Katherine Mortimer, by whom he had fifteen children.
Lucia de Beauchamp, married Robert de Napton.
John de Beauchamp, Lord Beauchamp KG (1315 – 2 December 1360), carried the royal standard at the Battle of Crecy
Elizabeth de Beauchamp (c. 1316–1359), married in 1328, Thomas Astley, 3rd Lord Astley, by whom she had a son William Astley, 4th Lord Astley.
Following the sudden death of Guy de Beauchamp at Warwick Castle on 28 July 1315, which was rumoured to have been caused by poisoning, Alice married thirdly on 26 October 1316, William la Zouche de Mortimer, 1st Lord Zouche de Mortimer (see Baron Zouche), by whom she had a son and daughter:

Alan la Zouche de Mortimer. (born 15 September 1317), participated in the Battle of Crécy, and died shortly afterwards.
Joyce la Zouche de Mortimer (born 1318)
Death[edit]
Alice de Toeni died on 1 January 1324/25.[3] The de Toeni lands and manors passed to her eldest son Thomas de Beauchamp, 11th Earl of Warwick. Her widower, Lord Zouche, later abducted and married Eleanor de Clare, widow of Hugh Le

zh.lifeSummary: Alice de Tosny是Ralph de Tosny和Mary的女兒，於1284年4月26日出生在英國賀特福爾福拉姆斯亞德。她於1305年之前與Guy de Beauchamp 10th Earl of Warwick結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1324年11月7日在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾沃里克沃里克 卡素享年40歲，埋葬在英國賀特福爾福拉姆斯亞德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tosny
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean Pierre.
Americanized form of any of various surnames ultimately derived from the Latin personal name Antonius (see Anthony ), for example Greek Antoniou and Italian Toni .
French: from the personal name Tony, a pet form of Antonin (from Latin Antoninus, a derivative of Antonius; see Anthony ).

variants.name: Anthony
meanings: English and West Indian (mainly Antigua and Barbuda, Saint Lucia, and Trinidad and Tobago, also Dutch Caribbean): from the personal name Anthony, Latin Antonius, which, with its variants and cognates, is one of the commonest personal names in Europe. Spellings with -h-, which first appear in English in the 16th century and in French (as Anthoine) at about the same time, are due to the erroneous belief that the name derives from Greek anthos ‘flower’. The popularity of the personal name in Christendom is largely due to the cult of the Egyptian hermit Saint Anthony ( AD 251–356), who in his old age gathered a community of hermits around him, and for that reason is regarded by some as the founder of monasticism. It was further increased by the fame of Saint Anthony of Padua (1195–1231), who long enjoyed a great popular cult and who is believed to help people find lost things. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and derivatives (patronymics) from other languages, for example Greek patronymic Antoniades , Italian Antoni , Polish Antoniewicz , Croatian and Serbian Antonović (see Antonovich ) and Antunović; see also below. The name Anthony is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Antony .

German, Flemish, and French (mainly Alsace): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from local equivalents of the Latin personal name Antonius, from its genitive form Antoni(i). In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the German, Dutch, French, and Slovak cognates Antoni 1 and Antony 2.
History: John Anthony of Hampstead, Middlesex, England (now part of north London) migrated to Boston, MA, in 1634. By 1640 he had moved to Providence, RI, where his descendants are still established.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: Alice de Tosny was born on 26 April 1284, in Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England as the daughter of Ralph de Tosny and Mary. She married Guy de Beauchamp 10th Earl of Warwick before 1305. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 7 November 1324, in Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 40, and was buried in Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Alice de Tosny è nata il il 26 aprile 1284 nel Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England come figlia di Ralph de Tosny e Mary. Lei sposato Guy de Beauchamp 10th Earl of Warwick prima del 1305. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 7 novembre 1324, nel Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 40 anni, e fu sepolta a Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice de Tosnyは1284年4月26日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアフラムステアドでRalph de TosnyとMaryの娘として生まれた。1305年以前にGuy de Beauchamp 10th Earl of Warwickと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1324年11月7日にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアワーウィックワーウィック カストルで40歳で死去し，イギリスハートフォードシュアイアフラムステアドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice de Tosny(은)는 1284년 4월 26일 영국 허트퍼드실르 플램스테아드에서 Ralph de Tosny(와)과 Mary의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1305년 이전 Guy de Beauchamp 10th Earl of Warwick(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1324년 11월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 워윅 워윅 카스틀에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허트퍼드실르 플램스테아드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Alice de Tosny nasceu em 26 de abril de 1284, em Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra filha de Ralph de Tosny e Mary. Ela casou-se com Guy de Beauchamp 10th Earl of Warwick antes 1305. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 7 de novembro de 1324, em Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 40 anos, e foi sepultada em Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Alice de Tosny родилась 26 апреля 1284 г. в местности флэмстид, хиртфордшир, Англия. Её родителей звали Ralph de Tosny и Mary. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guy de Beauchamp 10th Earl of Warwick перед 1305 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 7 ноября 1324 г. в местности варвикк кастл, варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 40 лет и была похоронена в местности флэмстид, хиртфордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Alice de Tosny nació el 26 de abril de 1284, en Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra como hija de Ralph de Tosny y Mary. Se casó con Guy de Beauchamp 10th Earl of Warwick antes de 1305. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 7 de noviembre de 1324, en Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 40 años, y fue sepultada en Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.
